pr.title: Match several ft, pl, and if functions
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: PR #2578 matched several functions across ftCo guard, plattack, and if_2FC93. The PR body claims five matched functions: ftCo_80091BC4, ftCo_80092158, ftCo_80093790, plAttack_8003759C, and un_802FE260, plus an improvement to fn_802FDA78 using the same file-local struct typing. The bot report corroborates six new matches including those five plus if_2FC93 .sdata, and reports fn_802FDA78 improved to 99.25%.

key_files.role: Matched or improved ftCo guard functions: rewrote ftCo_80091BC4 angle smoothing, cleaned ftCo_80092158 color packing, and simplified ftCo_80093790 call sequence with a narrow stack padding local.
evidence_refs: diff: ftCo_80093790 adds u8 _[8] and simplifies body
bot_report: ftCo_80093790, ftCo_80092158, ftCo_80091BC4 new 100% matches

key_files.role: Matched un_802FE260 and improved fn_802FDA78 by using a file-local struct pointer, signed sentinel storage, const slot data, explicit material color component writes, and narrower counter types.
evidence_refs: diff: un_804A1F10.x31 changed from unsigned char to signed char
diff: un_804DDBA8 changed to static char const
diff: fn_802FDA78 adds struct un_804A1F10_t* p and component diffuse writes
diff: un_802FE260 adds struct pointer p
bot_report: un_802FE260 new 100% match; fn_802FDA78 improved 82.48% to 99.25%; .sdata new 100% match

key_files.role: Matched plAttack_8003759C by factoring repeated attack-stat clearing into a static inline helper and using full-range zeroing loops.
evidence_refs: diff: plAttack_8003759C calls helper for attacks, hits, and x358_hits

what_changed.text: Matched five named functions according to the PR body: ftCo_80091BC4, ftCo_80092158, ftCo_80093790, plAttack_8003759C, and un_802FE260.
ftCo_80091BC4 was rewritten from helper-based normalizeAngle180/normalizeAngle0 code into explicit local calculations for stick angle, guard angle delta, smoothed guard angle, and stick magnitude.
ftCo_80092158 replaced M2C_FIELD and M2C_BITWISE-based generated-looking accesses with indexed color bytes from Fighter_804D650C and direct RGB packing for efSync_Spawn.

ftCo_80093790 removed an extra nested scope and temporary jobj local, calling ftCo_80092158 directly with the parts joint expression; it also introduced an 8-byte zeroed local array likely as a stack-shape tactic.

if_2FC93 changed un_804A1F10.x31 to signed char so -1 can be used directly as the sentinel instead of 0xFF, made un_804DDBA8 const, and used a local struct pointer p for repeated accesses in fn_802FDA78 and un_802FE260.

fn_802FDA78 changed material diffuse assignment from whole-struct assignment to explicit r/g/b/a component writes through HSD_MObj locals and improved substantially but did not fully match according to the bot report.
plAttack_8003759C factored three repeated stat-reset blocks into clearAttackStats and changed loops over StatsAttack_Count to begin at 0 in the helper and x504 reset loop.

smart_moves.text: Factoring the three repeated plAttack stat-reset blocks into a static inline helper reduced duplication and produced a reported 100% match for plAttack_8003759C.

Using a local pointer to the file-local un_804A1F10 struct reduced repeated global-looking accesses and was explicitly called out in the PR body as the typing approach used for un_802FE260 and fn_802FDA78.
evidence_refs: diff: struct un_804A1F10_t* p in fn_802FDA78 and un_802FE260

smart_moves.text: Changing x31 to signed char made the sentinel value readable as -1 and aligned the code with direct signed comparisons in fn_802FDA78.
Replacing generated M2C_FIELD byte loads with array indexing in ftCo_80092158 made the color packing source clearer while still corresponding to a reported new 100% match.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When several adjacent struct instances or fields are reset the same way, try a small static inline helper before keeping copied reset blocks; in this PR, clearAttackStats replaced three repeated blocks and plAttack_8003759C reached a reported 100% match.

For file-local aggregate state, introducing a local pointer to the struct can improve codegen and readability when many accesses target the same object; this PR used struct un_804A1F10_t* p in both un_802FE260 and fn_802FDA78.
evidence_refs: diff: p = &un_804A1F10 in fn_802FDA78 and un_802FE260
bot_report: un_802FE260 new 100% match; fn_802FDA78 improved

decomp_lessons.lesson: If a byte field uses -1 as a sentinel, recovering signedness can make the source form clearer and may be important to matching; here x31 changed from unsigned char to signed char and comparisons became p->x31 == -1.

Generated M2C_FIELD byte extraction can often be replaced by ordinary indexed byte access when the base object and stride are understood; ftCo_80092158 used Fighter_804D650C + offset and color[0..2] RGB packing instead of M2C_FIELD.

A near-matching function may require explicit scalar field writes instead of whole-struct assignment; fn_802FDA78 writes diffuse.r/g/b/a separately for each material and improved from 82.48% to 99.25%.

naming_conventions.name: clearAttackStats
naming_conventions.convention: Use a short verb-object helper name for a repeated local reset operation when the role is evident from the containing type and callsites.
evidence_refs: diff: static inline void clearAttackStats(struct plAttackStats* s)

naming_conventions.name: p
naming_conventions.convention: A very short local pointer name was used for repeated access to a file-local singleton struct; this is observed in the PR, not a global naming rule.

naming_conventions.name: color
naming_conventions.convention: Use a semantic local name for a byte-table pointer when its role is clear from use as RGB color data.
evidence_refs: diff: RGB packing with color[0], color[1], color[2]

naming_conventions.name: stick_rad, stick_deg, deg_delta, guard_deg, smoothed_deg, stick_mag
naming_conventions.convention: For angle smoothing math, locals named by unit and role improved readability versus temporary or reused names.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use a small static inline helper to recover repeated source structure while preserving callsite inlining.
evidence_refs: diff: clearAttackStats added and called three times
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: Grounded by bot-reported match for this PR; do not assume all repeated blocks should be helperized without local style and objdiff evidence.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Recover byte-table indexing and bit packing instead of keeping M2C_FIELD/M2C_BITWISE generated output.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: Requires evidence for stride and element layout; this PR uses offset = Player_GetUnk45(...) << 2 and color[0..2].

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use an explicitly typed file-local struct pointer for repeated singleton state access.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: fn_802FDA78 improved but did not fully match in bot report, so this tactic is supported as useful here but not proven sufficient alone.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Adjust signedness of a byte field to express a -1 sentinel directly.
evidence_refs: diff: unsigned char x31 to signed char x31
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: Signedness changes are type facts and should be backed by use sites or matching evidence.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Introduce a narrow dummy stack local for stack shape.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: This is a matching tactic, not style guidance. Existing standards require explicit verifier evidence and narrow scope for PAD_STACK/dummy-local-like tactics.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use explicit component assignments where whole-struct assignment does not produce the desired codegen.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: Evidence shows improvement, not full function match.

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.text: The loaded review record shows an APPROVED review with an empty body and no inline review comments.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: review by jurrejelle state APPROVED

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: Bot report recorded +2420 matched-code bytes, +16 matched-data bytes, six new matches, and six improvements in unmatched items.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching ftCo_80092158 or Fighter_804D650C color table typing to see whether the byte table gained a real struct or named color type.
Search for later if_2FC93 work on un_804A1F10_t, fn_802FDA78, x31 signedness, and un_804DDBA8 const ownership.
Search for clearAttackStats or plAttackStats reset helpers to see whether this helper pattern stayed local or was generalized.
Search for dummy stack locals named '_' or u8 _[8] in matched functions and collect verifier-backed examples versus rejected uses.
Search for material diffuse component-write changes in HSD_MObj/HSD_Material codegen matches.

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR identity, state, author, changed files, counts
human_pr_text: matched function claims and verification commands
raw_diff: complete diff excerpt for three changed files
text_corpus: bot report and approval review
changed_files: file list and line counts
notes: The bot report corroborates function match outcomes and score changes, but the loaded evidence does not include raw objdiff output.
The PR body lists verification commands, but loaded evidence does not prove command logs beyond the bot report.
No inline reviewer comments are available; review feedback is limited to an empty approved review and bot report.
The dummy u8 _[8] local in ftCo_80093790 is interpreted only as a likely stack-shape tactic because the diff shows no semantic use; this should not be promoted as accepted style.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Helperize repeated stat reset blocks when local evidence supports it
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2578, plAttack_8003759C reached a reported 100% match after three repeated plAttackStats reset blocks were replaced with a static inline clearAttackStats helper and called for attacks, hits, and x358_hits.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use typed byte-table indexing instead of M2C_FIELD when layout is evident
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2578, ftCo_80092158 replaced M2C_FIELD/M2C_BITWISE output with an offset into Fighter_804D650C and explicit color[0..2] RGB packing, producing a reported 100% match.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Signed sentinel fields can unlock clearer matching source
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2578, if_2FC93 changed un_804A1F10.x31 from unsigned char to signed char so code could compare and assign -1 directly in fn_802FDA78 and un_802FE260.

source_update_candidates.title: if_2FC93 uses file-local un_804A1F10_t state for UI object setup/update
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2578 provides evidence that src/melee/if/if_2FC93.c has a file-local un_804A1F10_t aggregate used by un_802FE260 and fn_802FDA78; a local pointer to this struct was used to match/improve these functions, and x31 acts as a signed -1 sentinel in this slice.
evidence_refs: bot_report: un_802FE260 new 100% match; fn_802FDA78 improved
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential path-specific fact for future if_2FC93 work; curator should verify against current source before adding.

source_update_candidates.title: Fighter_804D650C is indexed as 4-byte color entries in ftCo_80092158
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2578's ftCo_80092158 computes offset = Player_GetUnk45(player_id) << 2, points color at Fighter_804D650C + offset, and packs color[0], color[1], and color[2] into an RGB integer for efSync_Spawn.
source_update_candidates.reason: May be useful for future typing of Fighter_804D650C, but the PR does not introduce a named struct or global data type.

source_update_candidates.title: Document narrow handling of dummy stack locals
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2578 includes u8 _[8] = { 0 } in ftCo_80093790 alongside a reported 100% match. If not already covered, standards could remind contributors that dummy stack locals require narrow verifier evidence and should be treated as matching tactics, not source style.
source_update_candidates.reason: Existing matching-tactics standard already covers dummy locals broadly; curator may decide no update is needed or add this as an example.

rejection_notes: Do not generalize the u8 _[8] local into accepted style; evidence supports only that it appeared in this matched PR.
Do not claim fn_802FDA78 matched; bot report says it improved to 99.25%, not 100%.
Do not infer semantic names for un_804A1F10 fields beyond x31 signed sentinel behavior shown in the diff.
Do not treat .sdata/.sdata2 score changes as broad data ownership guidance; the PR was primarily code matching and only small data changes are shown.
